Emil Michael, now a senior Pentagon official, says he’ll never forgive Uber investors who ousted him and Kalanick
articles.description: When asked whether he was still "salty" about about being shown the door at Uber, alongside co-founder Travis Kalanick, Michael didn't equivocate. "I'll never forget that, nor forgive," he said.

articles.title: Bengaluru food delivery startup Swish raises $38M, its third round in 18 months
articles.description: Swish has more than doubled its valuation in a year as its full-stack, hyperlocal model positions ultra-fast food delivery as a high-frequency habit.

articles.title: Despite bitter rivalry, Kalshi, Polymarket CEOs back $35M predictions markets VC fund
articles.description: As prediction markets explode, the new firm called 5(c) Capital, will back startups supporting the burgeoning category.

articles.title: Air Street becomes one of the largest solo VCs in Europe with $232M fund
articles.description: London’s Air Street Capital has raised a large Fund III with eyes locked on backing early-stage European and North American AI companies.

articles.title: Apple Maps may be about to get ads
articles.description: Apple Maps may soon serve ads in top results when users search on terms like "restaurants."

articles.title: Someone has publicly leaked an exploit kit that can hack millions of iPhones
articles.description: Leaked "DarkSword" exploits published to GitHub allow hackers and cybercriminals to target iPhone users running old versions of iOS with spyware, according to cybersecurity researchers.

articles.title: Zipline snaps up another $200M to fuel its drone delivery expansion
articles.description: The funding adds to a previous round, announced in January,  that valued the drone startup at $7.6 billion.

articles.title: Bernie Sanders’ AI ‘gotcha’ video flops, but the memes are great
articles.description: Sen. Bernie Sanders thinks he's tricked Claude into revealing the AI industry's secrets, but he really just exposed how agreeable chatbots can become.

articles.title: Russian authorities block paywall removal site Archive.today
articles.description: A notice on the popular paywall-bypass website Archive.today said that access is blocked "by decision of [Russian] public authorities."

articles.title: Bipartisan bill seeks to ban sports betting on Kalshi and Polymarket
articles.description: Since FanDuel and DraftKings are subject to state-by-state gambling laws, rather than federal law, they would not be impacted by the bill.

articles.title: Vibe-coding startup Lovable is on the hunt for acquisitions
articles.description: Lovable's founder said the fast-growing vibe-coding startup is looking for startups and teams to join its company.

articles.title: Apple sets June date for WWDC 2026, teasing ‘AI advancements’
articles.description: Apple will host its next Worldwide Developers Conference the week of June 8. The company is expected to announce major updates to Siri with advanced AI capabilities.

articles.title: DoorDash introduces relief payments for drivers as the Iran-US war drives up gas prices
articles.description: Gas is one of the largest expenses for delivery drivers. With prices continuing to surge, DoorDash is offering a lifeline to Dashers with a new relief program.

articles.title: Leonid Radvinsky, the owner of  OnlyFans, has passed away
articles.description: OnlyFans owner Leonid Radvinsky has passed away at the age of 43 after a battle with cancer.

articles.title: Startup Gimlet Labs is solving the AI inference bottleneck in a surprisingly elegant way
articles.description: Gimlet Labs just raised an $80 million Series A for tech that lets AI run across NVIDIA, AMD, Intel, ARM, Cerebras and d-Matrix chips, simultaneously.

articles.title: Littlebird raises $11M for its AI-assisted ‘recall’ tool that reads your computer screen
articles.description: Littlebird is building an AI that reads your screen in real time to capture context, answer questions, and automate tasks, without relying on screenshots.

articles.title: Grab to buy Foodpanda Taiwan from Delivery Hero for $600 million
articles.description: Grab will acquire Delivery Hero’s Foodpanda business in Taiwan for $600 million, expanding beyond Southeast Asia and setting up a closer challenge to Uber Eats in a tightly contested market.

articles.title: Elizabeth Warren calls Pentagon’s decision to bar Anthropic ‘retaliation’
articles.description: In a letter to Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, Senator Elizabeth Warren (D-MA) equated the DOD's decision to label Anthropic a "supply-chain risk" as retaliation, arguing that the Pentagon could simply have terminated its contract with the AI lab.

digest:
## 🔬 Major Technical Developments

### iPhone 17 Pro Demonstrated Running a 400B LLM
URL: https://twitter.com/anemll/status/2035901335984611412
On-device inference crosses the threshold from demo to practical
Running a 400B parameter model on consumer mobile hardware signals a fundamental shift in where AI compute happens. This has implications for privacy, latency, and the economics of inference—if edge devices can run frontier-scale models, the hyperscaler inference moat erodes faster than expected. Watch for Apple to leverage this at WWDC.

### RocksDB development finds a CPU bug
URL: http://rocksdb.org/blog/2026/02/17/cpu-bug.html
Database stress testing uncovers silicon-level defects
When your storage engine surfaces CPU bugs, it's either a testament to your testing rigor or a warning about hardware reliability at scale. Meta's RocksDB team catching this matters for anyone running high-throughput workloads on affected chips—expect quiet firmware updates and louder questions about chip validation processes.

### Gimlet Labs is solving the AI inference bottleneck in a surprisingly elegant way
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/23/startup-gimlet-labs-is-solving-the-ai-inference-bottleneck-in-a-surprisingly-elegant-way/
Heterogeneous compute orchestration raises $80M Series A
Gimlet's approach—running inference across NVIDIA, AMD, Intel, ARM, Cerebras, and d-Matrix simultaneously—addresses the coming chip diversification that enterprises need as NVIDIA supply remains constrained. The $80M validates that the market sees inference orchestration as a real category, not a feature.

## 💼 Business & Market Signals

### FCC updates covered list to include foreign-made consumer routers
URL: https://www.fcc.gov/document/fcc-updates-covered-list-include-foreign-made-consumer-routers
Regulatory hammer falls on TP-Link and friends
The FCC denying certification to foreign-made consumer routers is protectionism with national security wrapping. This creates immediate supply chain disruption for retail networking but opens a wedge for domestic manufacturers and creates M&A opportunities for U.S.-based networking plays. Watch Netgear and Ubiquiti.

### Grab to buy Foodpanda Taiwan from Delivery Hero for $600 million
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/23/grab-to-buy-foodpanda-taiwan-from-delivery-hero-for-600-million/
Southeast Asian super-app makes first move beyond ASEAN
Grab paying $600M for Taiwan marks geographic expansion into a developed market where Uber Eats has meaningful presence. Delivery Hero continues shedding assets to focus capital—this is consolidation playing out exactly as the unit economics of food delivery demand. Taiwan becomes the test case for Grab's exportability.

### Air Street becomes one of the largest solo VCs in Europe with $232M fund
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/23/air-street-becomes-one-of-the-largest-solo-vcs-in-europe-with-232m-fund/
Nathan Benaich scales solo GP model for AI-focused deployment
A $232M Fund III for a solo GP is exceptional and reflects LP appetite for concentrated AI exposure through an operator with genuine technical credibility. Air Street's thesis-driven approach targeting early-stage AI in Europe and North America positions it to capture the next cohort of foundational AI infrastructure companies.

### Zipline snaps up another $200M to fuel its drone delivery expansion
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/23/zipline-snaps-up-another-200m-to-fuel-its-drone-delivery-expansion/
Drone logistics leader adds to $7.6B valuation round
Zipline stacking $200M on top of January's raise signals either aggressive expansion plans or a bridge to going public. At $7.6B, they're the clear category leader in autonomous delivery—the question is whether regulatory tailwinds in the U.S. match their Africa and healthcare traction.

## 📋 Case Studies & Deep Dives

### Someone has publicly leaked an exploit kit that can hack millions of iPhones
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/23/someone-has-publicly-leaked-an-exploit-kit-that-can-hack-millions-of-iphones/
"DarkSword" GitHub leak commoditizes iOS exploitation
Public availability of working iOS exploits targeting older versions changes the threat landscape from state-sponsored to script-kiddie accessible. Enterprises with BYOD policies and lagging MDM enforcement face immediate exposure. This will accelerate Apple's push on forced updates and iOS fragmentation cleanup.

### The Resolv hack: How one compromised key printed $23M
URL: https://www.chainalysis.com/blog/lessons-from-the-resolv-hack/
Single private key compromise enables eight-figure theft
Chainalysis dissecting a $23M hack down to one compromised key is a masterclass in why key management remains DeFi's Achilles heel. The forensics here matter less than the pattern—sophisticated protocols still failing on basic operational security. Institutional adoption of on-chain products continues to demand better custody solutions.

### I built an AI receptionist for a mechanic shop
URL: https://www.itsthatlady.dev/blog/building-an-ai-receptionist-for-my-brother/
SMB automation moves from concept to deployed production
This is the AI deployment story that matters more than enterprise case studies—a developer building a working voice AI receptionist for a family mechanic shop. The vibe-coding-to-production pipeline is now accessible enough for niche vertical solutions. This pattern will repeat across thousands of SMB categories.

## ⚡ Signal vs. Noise

The real story this week is compute diversification hitting inflection points across the stack—from 400B models running on mobile silicon to Gimlet orchestrating inference across six different chip architectures. Meanwhile, the FCC router decision and Anthropic-Pentagon friction show that geopolitics is now a first-order variable in tech strategy, not a background consideration. The prediction markets VC fund and Lovable's acquisition hunt suggest we're entering a phase where category winners are consolidating before the next liquidity window opens.
